goal: Close the stub-over-stdio gap left by WO-0040: extend `ucil-daemon mcp` to accept an optional `--repo <PATH>` flag that bootstraps a SQLite `KnowledgeGraph`, runs the existing tree-sitter → KG ingestion pipeline over every supported source file under the repo, and hands the populated KG to `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(...)` so `find_definition`, `find_references`, `get_conventions`, and `search_code` over stdio MCP return REAL file:line data instead of the `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub envelope. When `--repo` is absent the dispatch falls back to the WO-0040 stub path (backward-compatible).
plan_summary: Three changes. (1) `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs` grows a tiny hand-rolled arg parser that walks `std::env::args().skip(2)` looking for `--repo <VALUE>` OR `--repo=<VALUE>` (any other positional like `--stdio` is accepted-and-ignored to preserve WO-0040's CLI shape). When `--repo` resolves to an existing directory, the `mcp` arm opens a fresh `KnowledgeGraph` at a `tempfile::TempDir`-owned `knowledge.db`, iterates `.rs` / `.py` / `.ts` / `.tsx` / `.js` / `.jsx` / `.go` / `.java` / `.c` / `.cpp` files under the repo (skipping `target/`, `node_modules/`, `.git/`, `.ucil/`, and dotfiles), calls `ucil_daemon::executor::IngestPipeline::ingest_file` on each (errors per-file are logged at `warn!` and swallowed so one broken file does not kill the whole ingest), then wraps the populated KG in `Arc::new(Mutex::new(kg))` and constructs `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(kg_arc)`. The `TempDir` is moved into a `Box<dyn Drop>`-shaped holder so the SQLite file outlives the serve loop. If `--repo` is absent or its value is not an existing directory, fall through to the WO-0040 path (`McpServer::new()`). Tracing still routes to `std::io::stderr` in both `mcp` paths. (2) A new helper `fn walk_supported_source_files(root: &std::path::Path) -> Vec<std::path::PathBuf>` lives in main.rs (or in a sibling `crates/ucil-daemon/src/bin_ingest.rs`-style submodule only if the LOC budget demands) — pure std, recursive, deterministic (sorted output), capped at a defensive 50 000-file ceiling to prevent a gigantic monorepo from hanging the stdio transport startup. (3) A new integration test `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/e2e_mcp_with_kg.rs` spawns the binary via `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_ucil-daemon")` with `mcp --stdio --repo <abs-path-to-tests/fixtures/rust-project>` (the absolute path computed from `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")`), replays the `initialize` + `tools/call find_definition {name:"evaluate"}` handshake, parses the stdout responses, and asserts: `result._meta.tool == "find_definition"`, `result._meta.source == "tree-sitter+kg"`, `result._meta.found == true`, `result._meta.file_path` ends with `src/util.rs`, and `result._meta.start_line` is a positive integer. This proves end-to-end that the binary + main.rs dispatch + ingest + server + KG are wired correctly over the real stdio JSON-RPC transport — no in-process shortcuts. The test does NOT touch docker, does NOT spawn Serena, does NOT spawn any LSP server.
scope_in: crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs — extend the `Some("mcp")` arm with a `--repo <PATH>` / `--repo=<PATH>` arg parser built on `std::env::args().skip(2)` (NO clap / structopt / argh / pico-args dep added). On a valid `--repo` value, open `KnowledgeGraph::open(&kg_path)` where `kg_path` is `TempDir::new()?.path().join("knowledge.db")`, build an `IngestPipeline::new()`, call `walk_supported_source_files(&repo)` and `pipeline.ingest_file(&mut kg, &file)` for each result (use `Result::ok()` per file so individual failures log via `tracing::warn!(?file, error = %e, "ingest_file failed — skipping")` without aborting the ingest). After the loop, wrap in `Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(kg))`, move the `TempDir` into a holder bound to the same `match` arm lifetime, construct `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(kg_arc)`, and `.serve(tokio::io::stdin(), tokio::io::stdout()).await` with the existing `.context("ucil-daemon mcp --stdio: serve loop terminated with error")` wrapper. If `--repo` is absent or the path does not exist / is not a directory, keep the existing WO-0040 path (`McpServer::new().serve(...)`). Tracing subscriber still uses `.with_writer(std::io::stderr).init()` so stdout stays pristine for JSON-RPC framing — this is non-negotiable and the acceptance criteria greps for it. The non-`mcp` default arm is unchanged. Log the repo path + ingest file count at `tracing::info!(repo=?..., ingested=..., "ucil-daemon mcp --stdio bootstrap complete")` right before serve — this line appears on stderr only, never stdout.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs — add `fn walk_supported_source_files(root: &std::path::Path) -> Vec<std::path::PathBuf>` (pure std, recursive). Accepted extensions: `rs`, `py`, `ts`, `tsx`, `js`, `jsx`, `go`, `java`, `c`, `cpp`, `cc`, `h`, `hpp` (mirrors `ucil_treesitter`'s language coverage from P1-W2-F01). Skipped directories at ANY depth (matched by name, case-sensitive): `target`, `node_modules`, `.git`, `.ucil`, `dist`, `build`, `venv`, `.venv`, `__pycache__`. Skipped names starting with `.` at the repo-root-relative path (dotfile paths). Output sorted by `PathBuf` for determinism so the same repo yields the same ingest order across runs (important for the KG's insertion-order-dependent `ON CONFLICT` semantics documented in executor.rs line 452-454). Implementation: a `Vec<PathBuf>` accumulator + `fn visit(dir: &Path, out: &mut Vec<PathBuf>, count: &mut usize)` recursive helper, with a hard cap at 50_000 files (once reached, return early and log `tracing::warn!(cap=50000, "file discovery capped — repo too large for one-shot ingest")`). No `walkdir` dep added — plain `std::fs::read_dir`.

crates/ucil-daemon/tests/e2e_mcp_with_kg.rs — NEW integration-test file. `fn e2e_mcp_stdio_with_repo_returns_real_find_definition()` is a `#[test]` (synchronous, mirrors `e2e_mcp_stdio.rs`). Compute the fixture absolute path: `let fixture = std::path::PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")).parent().unwrap().parent().unwrap().join("tests/fixtures/rust-project")` (asserts `fixture.is_dir()`). Spawn `Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_ucil-daemon")).arg("mcp").arg("--stdio").arg("--repo").arg(&fixture).stdin/stdout(Stdio::piped()).stderr(Stdio::null())`. Write three JSON-RPC lines to stdin (each newline-terminated, matching the existing e2e_mcp_stdio pattern): `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize",...}`, `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/list","params":{}}`, `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"find_definition","arguments":{"name":"evaluate"}}}`. Close stdin. Wait for the process via `child.wait_with_output()`. For each stdout line, parse as `serde_json::Value` and find the one whose `id == 3`. Assert: `resp["result"]["_meta"]["tool"] == "find_definition"`, `resp["result"]["_meta"]["source"] == "tree-sitter+kg"`, `resp["result"]["_meta"]["found"] == true`, `resp["result"]["_meta"]["file_path"]` is a string that ends with `src/util.rs`, and `resp["result"]["_meta"]["start_line"]` is a positive integer > 0. Also assert `resp["result"]["_meta"].get("not_yet_implemented")` is None — proves the stub path is NOT taken. Test MUST NOT hard-code `start_line` to a literal (e.g. 128) because future fixture edits would break it; just assert `> 0`. Per DEC-0005, flat at module root — NO `mod tests { }` wrapper.

crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — add `tempfile = { workspace = true }` under `[dependencies]` ONLY if not already there; it IS already in `[dev-dependencies]` per Cargo.toml inspection but the `mcp --stdio --repo` code path runs in the release binary, so the dep MUST move (or be added to) `[dependencies]`. Executor's first action: inspect the current Cargo.toml; if `tempfile` is already under `[dependencies]`, no change. Otherwise, add it there and keep the `[dev-dependencies]` entry (or remove the dev-dep entry if Cargo de-duplicates).

Rustdoc on main — extend the existing crate-root `//!` block to document `--repo`: add a sentence such as `//! With \`--repo <PATH>\`, the "mcp" subcommand bootstraps a SQLite \`KnowledgeGraph\` by running the tree-sitter ingestion pipeline over the repo so \`find_definition\` returns real file:line data instead of the stub envelope.` Keep the rustdoc SHORT (two sentences max) — every prior rejection under `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` in this phase came from lightweight link references that cargo failed to resolve. Use fully qualified paths or plain backticks; do NOT reference internal crate items via shorthand like `KnowledgeGraph::open`.

Commit ladder — approximately 4 commits: (1) `feat(daemon): add --repo arg parser to mcp subcommand in main.rs` (~30 LOC: the arg-parse helper + the two new branches of the match); (2) `feat(daemon): add walk_supported_source_files helper in main.rs` (~60 LOC: the recursive walker + the 50k cap + extension allowlist); (3) `feat(daemon): bootstrap KnowledgeGraph + IngestPipeline when --repo present` (~50 LOC: the KG open + ingest loop + with_knowledge_graph wiring + tracing info line); (4) `test(daemon): e2e integration — spawn binary with --repo, assert real find_definition` (~100-150 LOC: the new tests/e2e_mcp_with_kg.rs file). A 5th optional commit `build(daemon): promote tempfile to [dependencies] for --repo code path` if Cargo.toml editing is required. Soft target ≤50 LOC/commit; the test-introduction commit MAY exceed under DEC-0005 precedent.
scope_out: NO persistent KG at `.ucil/shared/kg.db` — this WO uses a `TempDir`-owned per-invocation KG. The persistent-KG wiring (reading the `two-tier` storage layout from WO-0022 and reusing cached KG between invocations) is a future WO. Rationale: the stdio transport is invocation-scoped, and WO-0041's acceptance target is "real find_definition over stdio" — not "fast cold-start". Persistent-KG perf arrives in P1-W3-F08 follow-ups or Phase 2.

NO `--kg-path <PATH>` / `--session-id <UUID>` / `--branch <NAME>` flags — only `--repo <PATH>` is in scope. Adding more flags risks the single-subcommand hand-rolled parser growing into a full CLI framework dispute that DEC-0006 explicitly deferred.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` — the `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph` API (server.rs:396) is already stable and covered by `build_find_definition_fixture` + `test_find_definition_tool`. This WO calls into the existing surface unchanged.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` — `IngestPipeline` and `language_from_extension` are consumed as-is. `rust_project_fixture` is `pub(crate) #[cfg(test)]` and is NOT consumed by this WO (the binary does not need a fixture helper; the test computes the fixture path from `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`).
NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` — the binary imports `ucil_daemon::executor::IngestPipeline` via the existing `pub mod executor;` re-export. No new items need exposing.
NO changes to any OTHER crate: `ucil-core`, `ucil-treesitter`, `ucil-lsp-diagnostics`, `ucil-embeddings`, `ucil-agents`, `ucil-cli`.

NO changes to `scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh`, `scripts/verify/diagnostics-bridge.sh`, `scripts/verify/multi-lang-coverage.sh`, `scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh`, `scripts/gate/phase-1.sh`, or any other script — pyright framing in `diagnostics-bridge.sh` and the `multi-lang-coverage.sh` TODO body are harness triage Bucket-B items tracked separately in `ucil-build/escalations/20260419-0152-monitor-phase1-gate-red-integration-gaps.md`.

NO edit to `.claude/settings.json` — registering `mcpServers.ucil` for the effectiveness-evaluator is harness-config territory. This WO delivers the SOURCE side of the wire; the settings side is a user/triage action separately tracked.

NO feature-list flip expected — all 34 Phase-1 features are already `passes=true`. This WO's purpose is gate-closure AND effectiveness unblock, not feature delivery. `feature_ids: []` matches WO-0040 / WO-0025 / WO-0007 precedent.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, `it.skip`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.

NO parse of `--stdio`, `--help`, `--version`, etc. — the only flag recognised is `--repo`. `--stdio` is accepted-and-ignored to preserve WO-0040's CLI shape; any other unknown flag is ALSO accepted-and-ignored so the stdio transport never fails on a host that passes an extra CLI arg.

NO multi-language ingest beyond what `IngestPipeline::language_from_extension` already supports — the extension allowlist is informational (filters which files get FED TO ingest); the actual language support surface is `ucil-treesitter`'s responsibility and is unchanged here.
acceptance: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_with_kg` exits 0 with exactly one passing integration test (`e2e_mcp_stdio_with_repo_returns_real_find_definition`).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio` still exits 0 (the WO-0040 stub path regression guard stays green).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib` still exits 0 — no in-process regression in server / executor / session_manager / watcher suites.
`cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` emits no `^error` and no `^warning: unresolved` lines (rustdoc-link landmine guard).
`./target/debug/ucil-daemon mcp --stdio` (no `--repo`) still exits 0 on EOF with 0 bytes on stdout other than JSON responses to any inputs — the stub path is intact (WO-0040 regression guard).
`./target/debug/ucil-daemon` with no arguments still exits 0 under 1 s with no JSON on stdout.

Mutation check (verifier runs): stash the `with_knowledge_graph(kg_arc)` call in main.rs (replace with `McpServer::new()`) → `e2e_mcp_stdio_with_repo_returns_real_find_definition` fails (the `_meta.found == true` assertion trips); restore → green. Proves the new main.rs dispatch is the sole reason the assertion holds.

Effectiveness side-effect (informational, not required to flip): if the `.claude/settings.json` entry for `mcpServers.ucil` is separately registered to invoke `ucil-daemon mcp --stdio --repo <REPO>`, the `nav-rust-symbol` effectiveness scenario transitions from `skipped_tool_not_ready` to `OK` or `FAIL` on merit. This WO does NOT require that transition to flip — the source-side wiring is the deliverable.
acceptance_criteria: cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo build -p ucil-daemon --bin ucil-daemon --quiet 2>&1 | tee /tmp/build-WO-0041.log && test -x ./target/debug/ucil-daemon
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_with_kg 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-kg-WO-0041.log && grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed' /tmp/test-kg-WO-0041.log

cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-stub-WO-0041.log && grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed' /tmp/test-stub-WO-0041.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-lib-WO-0041.log && grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. [0-9]+ passed; 0 failed' /tmp/test-lib-WO-0041.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-ws-WO-0041.log && ! grep -Eq 'test result: FAILED' /tmp/test-ws-WO-0041.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy-WO-0041.log && ! grep -q '^error' /tmp/clippy-WO-0041.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps 2>&1 | tee /tmp/doc-WO-0041.log && ! grep -qE '^(error|warning: unresolved)' /tmp/doc-WO-0041.log

dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W3-F07: passes=true. WO-0010 landed `McpServer::serve(reader, writer)` at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` — consumed unchanged by this WO.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W4-F02: passes=true. `KnowledgeGraph::open(path)` + CRUD surface landed by WO-0011/0012/0020 — the binary opens a fresh KG per invocation via this API.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W4-F03: passes=true. `KnowledgeGraph::resolve_symbol` landed by WO-0031 — invoked internally by `handle_find_definition` when the KG is populated.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W4-F04: passes=true. `IngestPipeline::ingest_file` landed by WO-0032 (crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs:416) — the bulk-ingest loop in main.rs calls this unchanged.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W4-F05: passes=true. `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph` + `handle_find_definition` landed by WO-0033 — the real-data response envelope this WO's test asserts against.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W3-F07-stdio: WO-0040 landed the `mcp --stdio` subcommand dispatch in main.rs + the `e2e_mcp_stdio` test — this WO extends that dispatch without regressing the stub path.

lessons_applied: WO-0040's exact scope_out deferral: `NO KG bootstrap in mcp mode — McpServer::new() is correct for this WO ... End-to-end find_definition over stdio with real symbol data is a LATER WO`. This WO IS that later work.

WO-0040 stdout-pollution trap: tracing_subscriber in `mcp` mode MUST write to stderr. Carried forward verbatim: the new --repo code path also initialises tracing via `.with_writer(std::io::stderr).init()` before ANY ingest logging fires. Acceptance criteria greps for `with_writer` in main.rs.

WO-0009 / WO-0024 / WO-0025 / WO-0027 / WO-0039 rustdoc intra-doc-link rejections: acceptance_criteria includes mandatory `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` sub-check. Rustdoc additions stay SHORT and avoid internal-type shorthand. The crate-root rustdoc was RE-written in WO-0040 and is already under `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` — adding one more sentence has minimal risk if the sentence uses fully qualified paths or plain backticks only.

WO-0038 / WO-0039 grep-for-summary-line regex drift: acceptance_criteria uses `grep -Eq` with alternation that matches BOTH cargo-test (`test result: ok. N passed; 0 failed`) AND cargo-nextest (`N tests run: M passed`) summary-line shapes.

DEC-0011 coverage-gate vs nextest split: the new test uses `CARGO_BIN_EXE_ucil-daemon` and does not mutate PATH, so the `test_support::env_guard` / `PathRestoreGuard` pattern is NOT required — same as WO-0040.

WO-0024 / WO-0027 abandonment avoidance: this WO does NOT depend on docker, does NOT depend on Serena, does NOT depend on any external LSP server. The binary is spawned via `CARGO_BIN_EXE_ucil-daemon`, ingest uses the in-workspace fixture, and the KG is a TempDir SQLite file. CI-independent.
DEC-0005 module-root flat test style: new test is `#[test] fn e2e_mcp_stdio_with_repo_returns_real_find_definition()` at module root — NO `mod tests { }` wrapper. Matches WO-0040 pattern.

Empty feature_ids WO precedent: WO-0007 / WO-0025 / WO-0040 all shipped with empty `feature_ids` for gate-closure / fix work. `feature_ids: []` is legitimate. The verifier-side feature-flip step is skipped; the gate-side effectiveness/integration improvement is the deliverable.
CLI-parsing precedent from WO-0040: hand-rolled `std::env::args().nth(N).as_deref()` / `skip(N)` loops are the accepted pattern for narrow-scope argument parsing. No new CLI framework dep added.

IngestPipeline per-file error swallow: current `IngestPipeline::ingest_file` returns `ExecutorError::UnsupportedExtension` for files outside the language allowlist. This WO pre-filters via `walk_supported_source_files` extension list to keep the ingest-loop log quiet, and any residual per-file errors are `warn!`-logged rather than propagated so one broken file never aborts the full stdio startup. Matches the resilience pattern in executor.rs's own tests (WO-0032 asserts idempotency but never asserts full-repo ingest succeeds).

tempfile dep scope: WO-0040 kept `tempfile` in `[dev-dependencies]` (test-only). This WO's release binary imports `tempfile::TempDir` — executor must verify / promote the dep to `[dependencies]`. Explicit first step in scope_in.

Post-pass3-halt / drift-counter hygiene: WO-0041 is emitted post-WO-0040 merge (commit 42d3d61) with a valid progression through the planner→executor pipeline — not a drift-recovery emission. No ADR required since the CLI extension (`--repo`) is a narrow additive change within phase-1 scope.
